gleam 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Allocation Pipeline (Internal)

Source: R/run_allocation.R
run_allocation.Rd

Computes allocation fractions by calculating cohort energy for meat, milk, fibre, work and summarising to herd level. Returns cohort results, herd totals, and long-form shares for associating emissions with commodities.

run_allocation(
  allocation_inputs,
  allocation_type = "biophysical-energy",
  group_by_keys = c("ADM0_CODE", "HerdType_short", "Animal_short", "LPS_short"),
  standard_protein = 0.033,
  standard_fat = 0.04,
  standard_lactose = 0.048,
  ratio_ne_me_camelids = 0.43,
  assessment_duration = 365
)

Arguments

allocation_inputs

Cohort-level input table provided as data.frame or data.table.

allocation_type

Character vector that defines the allocation methodology in use. Default= "biophysical-energy"

group_by_keys

Character vector that lists the columns used for herd aggregation.

standard_protein

Numeric scalar for reference milk protein content (g per 100 g milk).

standard_fat

Numeric scalar for reference milk fat content (g per 100 g milk).

standard_lactose

Numeric scalar for reference milk lactose content (g per 100 g milk).

ratio_ne_me_camelids

Numeric scalar for the net-to-metabolizable energy conversion for camelids.

assessment_duration

Numeric scalar. Length of the assessment period (days).

Value

A named list of three data.table objects:

  • cohort_allocation_inputs: Cohort-level inputs to estimate allocation shares at herd-level.

  • allocation_long: Herd-level, long-format representation of allocation shares with commodity labels.

Details

The function implements biophysical allocation based on energy requirements to produce different products, following the IDF (2022) global carbon footprint standard for the dairy sector.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Load example input from the package and run allocation calculation
input_path <- system.file("extdata/GLEAM_input_allocation.csv", package = "gleam")
allocation_data <- data.table::fread(input_path)
result <- run_allocation(allocation_data)
# View herd-level allocation shares
head(result$herd_summary[, .(Animal_short, allocation_share_meat, allocation_share_milk)])
# View long-format allocation
head(result$allocation_long)
} # }

Contents

Developed by Ahmed Jou, Yassine Elaouni, Dominik Wisser, Lydia Lanzoni, Giuseppe Tempio, Federal Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Regional Identity of Germany BMELH.
